Hollywood publicist **Peggy Siegal** reveals in a forthcoming memoir that **Jeffrey Epstein** gave her a **$100,000 check** and discussed his search for a "baby mama," according to **Fox News**.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup>

The tell-all book contains what Fox News describes as "5 bombshells" about the financier, who died in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex trafficking charges.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> Siegal, known for orchestrating high-profile film premieres and Oscar campaigns, had social connections to Epstein before his arrest.

## The Baby Mama Search

Siegal writes in her memoir that Epstein "was always looking for a baby mama," Fox News reported.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The publicist describes conversations in which the financier discussed his desire to have children.

The source material does not specify when these conversations occurred or provide additional details about Epstein's stated plans.

## The $100,000 Gift

Epstein gave Siegal a **$100,000 check**, according to Fox News.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The report does not specify the year of the gift or its stated purpose.

Siegal's memoir describes her social relationship with Epstein, who maintained connections to entertainment industry figures through publicists and other intermediaries.

## Professional Fallout

The publicist faced professional consequences in Hollywood following Epstein's July 2019 arrest on federal sex trafficking charges. Media outlets reported at the time that Siegal's association with Epstein damaged her reputation in an industry where she had built a career facilitating connections between filmmakers, actors, and awards voters.

Epstein died in his Manhattan jail cell on August 10, 2019, while awaiting trial. He had been charged with sex trafficking of minors in Florida and New York. The New York City medical examiner ruled his death a suicide.

## Additional Memoir Revelations

Fox News describes the memoir as containing five "bombshells" about Epstein, including the baby mama search and the $100,000 check.<sup>[1](#ref-1)</sup> The source material does not detail the other three revelations.

Siegal's book represents a firsthand account from a member of Epstein's social circle. Her position as a Hollywood publicist gave her access to both entertainment figures and wealthy individuals like Epstein who sought connections to that world.

Epstein was convicted in 2008 on state charges of soliciting a minor in Florida. He served 13 months under a controversial plea agreement before federal prosecutors brought new charges in 2019.
